title: The World after 2I
wikitext:
[]: The World after 2I             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed Apr 30, 2008 3:55 am I found this interesting after reading the above image. Below are a couple of maps produced by the University of Toronto showing one view of sea-level rises due to polar ice melts. The figures are computer simulations of what happens to sea levels in the event of ice sheet melts near the Northern or Southern poles. The blue colours show a drop in sea level while the red and orange colours show a rise. The top image shows the impact on sea level when polar ice sheets in the Antarctic are melted. The blue colour indicates a decrease in sea level near the Antarctic while it also shows a corresponding rise in sea level in the Northern Hemisphere. The bottom image shows the effect on sea level when the polar ice sheets of Greenland are melted. The sea level decreases around Greenland but rises in the Southern Hemisphere. The image below shows the low areas on the Australian mainland and whilst sea levels will probably not rise to this extent it does highlight the areas that will be affected by flooding, storm surges, cyclones etc (these appear as green and blue). After looking at these images of the effects on Australia I can understand why over 2 billion people in the southern hemisphere where killed This is the world after 2I (exept for antartic) http://www.johnstonsarchive.net/spaceart/earthicefree.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Apr 30, 2008 8:32 am this ignores that the explosion of Second Impact not only melted Antarctica, but also changed Earth's axial tilt; both poles melted, these models are based on one or the other.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed Apr 30, 2008 8:34 am I respect that but still the world appears to have flooded the same way

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Apr 30, 2008 8:48 am I've considered using real-world global warming sea level change models to more or less guess what the post 2I world looks like however, to my knowledge they never actually state specifically how much the sea levels have risen. If they did, we could probably look up a similar model. ....DO any resources say just how much the sea levels have risen? ***The "NGE White Paper RPG" thing supposedly had a map of Japan's flooded coasts and info on this; does anyone have scans available?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Apr 30, 2008 8:56 am How is Australia's sea level rise relevant to NGE? I don't think any Evas were even produced there. What should Japan look like according to these folks? There are a few satellite shots that show Japan looking pretty whole, though we know Tokyo is underwater and is where the undersea action takes place (and there doesn't appear to be any land on the horizon) in Asuka Strikes! A similar topic came up here briefly - http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=156188#156188 The screenshot you posted is .5mb hueg. V wrote: The "NGE White Paper RPG" thing supposedly had a map of Japan's flooded coasts and info on this; does anyone have scans available?

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed Apr 30, 2008 9:03 am I was actualy basing the fact on the Tidalwave and how far I would it would have effected the southern hemisphere. The screenshot you posted is .5mb hueg.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Apr 30, 2008 9:49 am Zuggy wrote: There are a few satellite shots that show Japan looking pretty whole, though we know Tokyo is underwater and is where the undersea action takes place (and there doesn't appear to be any land on the horizon) in Asuka Strikes!

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Apr 30, 2008 11:35 am UrsusArctos wrote: That's Ito on the Izu peninsula, not Tokyo!

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Wed Apr 30, 2008 2:41 pm I remeber reading somewhere, that if the Greenland and Antarctic ice-sheets where to melt in their entirety, sea-levels would rise by approximately seventy metres. Here it is: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sea_level_rise#Glaciers_and_ice_caps Wikipedia wrote: If small glaciers and polar ice caps on the margins of Greenland and the Antarctic Peninsula melt, the projected rise in sea level will be around 0.5 m. Melting of the Greenland ice sheet would produce 7.2 m of sea-level rise, and melting of the Antarctic ice sheet would produce 61.1 m of sea level rise.[3] The collapse of the grounded interior reservoir of the West Antarctic Ice Sheet would raise sea level by 5-6 m.[4]

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Apr 30, 2008 3:18 pm Zuggy wrote: V wrote: The "NGE White Paper RPG" thing supposedly had a map of Japan's flooded coasts and info on this; does anyone have scans available?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 30, 2008 3:26 pm Yeah, V, it's from the Nerv White Paper Book. (That's probably a raped version of a scan I did, too. ...who the heck uses JPEG compression like that on a B&W image? Honestly! ) Zuggy wrote: UrsusArctos wrote: That's Ito on the Izu peninsula, not Tokyo!

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Apr 30, 2008 6:26 pm If it was off the coast of the Itoh peninsula, there wouldn't really be wrecked skyscrapers on the sea floor. I really take "on screen visual evidence" over "stuff written on a title card during a clip show recap summary" but then again....Tokyo-3 is kind of WEST of Odawara......Yokohama/Tokyo-1 would be a needless trip north up a bay they'd never need to go into. So....I suspect they might have just condensed the two sites "for fun" ***More probably, its just coastal ruins leftover from "Old" Odawara...... ....yeah they kind of condensed things here

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Apr 30, 2008 7:03 pm Who's to say that Post-2I Itoh didn't have skyscrapers, V? Reichu wrote: <snip>In an engagement off the shore of Old Itoh <snip> And, yeah, Zuggy is right about Yokohama. http://www.evacommentary.org/appendix/map_tokyo.jpg I have Yokohama circled already. Ito is visible to the southwest, all the way over on the Izu peninsula. (To the left of the "UP".) Did they mess up again? I mean, unless they magically folded space so that the sunken ruins of Yokohama were conveniently positioned off the coast of (Old) Ito for the duration of the battle.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu May 01, 2008 2:55 am UrsusArctos wrote: Who's to say that Post-2I Itoh didn't have skyscrapers?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu May 01, 2008 9:19 am Reichu wrote: That's probably a raped version of a scan I did, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Thu May 01, 2008 9:26 am This is it I saved it http://www.freewebs.com/projectretake/1172238893566.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu May 01, 2008 7:35 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Who's to say that Post-2I Itoh didn't have skyscrapers?

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu May 01, 2008 10:15 pm Hey, hey, you do have to account for the missing continent of Antarctica. Whatever gain in sea level you might potentially get from melting the entire ice cap would have to go back and fill in the underwater volume of the land there.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu May 01, 2008 10:20 pm Edit : I just made a boo boo.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu May 01, 2008 10:29 pm Sachi_13 wrote: CorporalChaos wrote: Whatever gain in sea level you might potentially get from melting the entire ice cap would have to go back and fill in the underwater volume of the land there.
